channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology — 70 replies — 279.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology

Open thread • Replies: 70 • Updated: 2026-06-19 16:27 UTC

OP

This will be one of the greatest investment opportunities in our lifetime within another decade or so.

Tech is stagnating, just as every other industry is out of viable optimization methodologies. There is nothing new being created, every niche is saturated and optimized. AI will produce some value but it is ultimately a giant bubble that will never, and I mean never advance to the point of consciousness or intelligence (unless they use biological brains and do so correctly - they will fail). Science doesn't even know where consciousness comes.

That being said: sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness) will be the big new technology that solves real world problems. And the funny thing is: tech companies will eventually run out of other means of generating increasing revenue and will essentially HAVE to go this route, just like they're currently doing with AI. They have nothing else. This technology will have vast and continuous demand as it will entirely replace the old/current paradigm.

Early replies (first 3)

62375864
>>62375553
I want sexbots to be real so bad

62375914
>>62375864
same
all the despair, pain and unhappiness i feel... if it was a good sexbot i would be happy.
the thing is, the first sexbots will be bad. like truly awful. probably not physically dangerous... maybe. but not enjoyable. like how electric masturbators are really good at making me nut, but a soulless experience that leaves me feeling empty. the technology to replace a living organism with a robot is just not there.
if it were viable, there would be news. most people who are lonely and horny want the sexbot. but porn and masturbators are the best we have. sexbots are an AI complete problem.

62375977
>>62375553
>sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness)
AI is not about creating consciousness. you are retarded if you think intelligence requires consciousness.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382457
>>62376404
They'll be fit only as servitors

62382461
>>62375553
>>62376084
>>62379333
>>62380759
>>62380948
op stfu and just give me the public company to ape into

62382474
>artificial womb

why do you want to be a single parent?

62382691
>>62382461
>op stfu
well now I'm not gonna

62382737
>>62382474
because no woman wants anon. no matter how much money anon makes.

62382774
>>62378763
>Women would still be mothers
Across all the AIslop you generated ITT I just had to laugh at this being a "point" made in a discussion where women would, in fact, no longer be mothers.

62382783
>>62382474
>>62382737
uh, what? I'm talking about an investing opportunity on the business board. I actually just got a message a few minutes ago on fb messenger from one of my sisters friends who's been trying to get with me for a year and my night feels ruined now because it was extremely annoying trying to reject her nicely on the basis of "trust me you do not want anything to do with me, I am autistic and do not like leaving the house" and I still had to ghost her completely. I don't date or have any desire to and I don't want to have kids at all. I am extremely handsome but also an autistic freak who... doesn't like to leave the house kek dating is not for me, I am for looking at only.

62382794
>>62382783
Can I look at your feet?

62382802
>>62382794
no thats gross and gay

62382809
thoughts?

62382812
>>62382802
You're for looking only and you won't even let us look? Fucking prude.

62382814
>>62382774
> new tech would be easily available to public and not only accessible by the wealthy
> the poors and religious fundamentalists wouldn’t continue to multiply like rabbits
Yeah, no more mothers, for sure, good point

62382816
>>62382809
That’s an AI video and very creepy

62382832
>>62376404
Truly horror. The audacity of these humans. It's not just humans they perform horrific and abominable procedures too but animals as well.

>>62377063
Trvke. A horror experiment from decades ago where they removed any sort of affection to a batch of babies. Just mechanical feeding via picking up the kid, no affection, and using a bottle. They quickly started dying off and the experiment ended.

62382834
>>62382814
In the context of "women would still hold their value leveraging this to gain more freedom" and using that as a suggestion of how such women would still hold value...

Yeah nah, the women "leveraging" this to obtain more "freedom" would not be mothers. That wouldn't be the go to source of value to anyone coherent trying to argue birthing was irrelevant to a woman's value. It's ultra retarded.

62382837
>>62375914
Just go somewhere you can hire a prostitute you fucking retard. Then when you realize pussy isn't that special, just buy a quality flesh light and move on with your life.
>>62376404
Thanks Anon, I fully intend to make an entire army of white children this way and your diatribe has made sure I will fight for access during incubation.

62382852
Some of you guys are so fucking stupid that you can not see what is happening right in front of you.

We are long past jerking each other off about the ethics and morality of things....if those ideas had any meaning, we would not allow girls to make more money than a fucking surgeon just by selling her bath water.

OP is fucking right - Robo pussy & artifical wombs is going to fucking send.

This is bitcoin when it was $12.76
This is Amazon when jeff was still living in new mexico selling books
This is Youtube when it had like only 1000 accounts that have registered on the site

I just had an old 55+ year old guy tell me the only reason women have value today is because men want whats between there legs. Throw that out and she's not worth the salt.

The world economic forum stated way back almost a decade ago THAT THIS ALONG WITH GENE-DNA EDITING is going to fucking send.

Think you dumb fuck "If robo pussy is bitcoin at $12.76....what does robo-pussy look like when its at a trillion dollar marketcap?"

62382854
>>62382834
I really want to know what woman ever saw motherhood as a path to freedom. Marriage, yes, but motherhood no.
It’s a desire built into them to want concern, similar to men, but freedom has nothing to do with it. Most women recognize the immediate responsibility and shackling to the child, it’s why motherhood age has increased.

The same women who are trophy wives would continue to be trophy wives because their value was never motherhood.
Haha these threads never cease to crack me up.
Where is this misconception that women lorded babies over men’s heads, and not men lording babies over other men’s heads? They’re the keepers of the babies or some shit not because or an avenue to freedom but because if they pick wrong it’s guaranteed misery

62383573
>>62375553
okay and what stock do I invest in?

62383663
>>62376404
get real, we live in a world where it's legal to kill babies and people call them parasites, trash parents with substance abuse issues poisoning kids before they're even born, most animals are better parents than human mothers, this will be an improvement.

62383804
>>62382448
>when hookers are literally right there and are real women
That's kind of the problem though. They're real women. With everything that goes with that, both good and bad. There's definitely demand for something between porn and sex robots, but I also wonder if there isn't something way more immersive and easier than sex robots possible to deliver. Maybe some type of VR experience, short of having to give Elon Musk control of your brain.

62385644
>>62382448
Depends where you live. In a lot of first world countries hooker are illegal and the ones you'll find are either ugly 40s chicks or they request $800 min for half an hour with them.

62386435
>>62375553
This is a bad idea because governments across the world already have laws coming down the pipe banning these incredibly dystopian technologies.

62386545
>>62375553
Sex robot is my hand. My artificial womb is my hand. I don't need to buy poorly made overpriced plastic shit made by jews to cope with my lonelyness

62386576
>>62386545

>Lonely
>Can't afford hookers
>Blames Jews

62386645
>>62386576
I can afford your mom but I grew tired of prostitutes over the years. Too much hassle, choosing a girl, making an appointment, get to her place, only to see a slampig whore reeking of pizza, fuck that. I'd rather watch some amy rose futa banging on rouge the bat and be done with my urges

62386707
>>62375553
I think you’re directionally correct but wrong about the details. I don’t see a huge market for artificial wombs in isolation, it’s not a lack of wombs that are leading to our societal problems, it’s that women as a whole are retards when it comes to shepherding their most valuable commodity: their eggs. So we would need an adjunct tech like IVG for our species to really transcend past a primitive state.

62387048
This thread is a great example of why /biz/ will never make it.

Sexbots/AI romantic companions are already a thing, and its a growing muliti-billion dollar industry. The sexdoll industry is also growing and is highly competitive on the higher end. One of the few cases where AI might actually be what its hyped up to be. All attraction is "coded", whether by evolution or by actual code.

Artificial wombs directly target the growing surrogacy market, where even in cheap jurisdictions like Columbia, the average surrogate agency charges $80k or more. US is more like $200k. This is prime territory for an artificial replacement. The single dad question is already being answered by the surrogacy industry, and their is increasing YoY demand from single men to become fathers.

What I would need to see is some kind of local AI capable of running the bot's mind and the body. It will likely cost $50k in about 10 years just to build the brain that can run the bot and run the brain. The bot will be expensive, but it could be financed on a 10 year term like lots of other luxury goods like exotic cars, boats and other shit. Or it can be part of a worker's compensation package for working in a remote environment like the Arctic for long periods of time.

62388341
>>62386645

>Can only afford fat ugly hookers
>Jerks off to futa faggotry
>Blames Jews

62388354
>>62375553
Milfs owe me sex.

There are male pornstars like Jordi el Niño Polla, Juan el Caballo Loco, Ricky Spanish, Jodie Johnson, Elias Cash, etc. That get to have sex with milfs all day while incels wageslave to death in the trades.

Milfs owe me sex for all the suffering I've had to endure.

item.title: How is your career going? — 71 replies — 179.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is your career going?

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-06-19 16:19 UTC

OP

I make $28/hr as a Fire Alarm Inspection Technician. I have 2 licenses and 4.5 years of experience:
-FASA/BASA
-NICET Inspection and Testing Level 2

Early replies (first 3)

62381055
Going for the NICET Fire Alarm Systems Level 1 license in the next 2 months or so. Will hopefully get to $30/hr.

62381060
>>62381049
*chirp*

62381078
>>62381049
is that a fun job? sounds comfy desu

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383096
>>62381049
I'm a warehouse grunt
$20 / hour
How do I even go up from here? Maybe a forklift license?

62383304
I make 31 an hour doing quality assurance for asphalt and I think about killing myself every day.

62383355
>>62381049
I make $29/hour driving the city bus.
I have a class B CDL.
While the pay is nothing special, the benefits are incredible, the hours are great, and the company overall is a dream to work for.
I plan on leeching every last cent I can out of this place before getting a fat retirement in 30 years.

62383464
>>62382153
It won't, everything has yearly inspections

62383470
Oh yeah its going fucking awful. I make $23/hr annoying people at their homes. I fully expect to be killed one day doing this but someone's gotta do it

62383481
$48 per hour? Thats $2000 per week. You can buy otm SPY contracts and double your weekly net. The issue is. Nobody want to work learn a life changing skill outside of their job because theyre so mentally exhausted from everyday distraction & life. We call ourselves the 1%'ers

62383500
>>62381049
late 20's, 120K in business analytics. It's an adult daycare but the pay's good, feels bad going in the office once a week tho. There are some qts around but that's just about all my social interaction for the week. My dream is to keep the same amount of pay but work in another country in another time zone, but that would be rough. Seriously thinking about it one day

62383513
i make $130k base + 15% bonus target as a corporate accounting manager. i'm underpaid by like $20k/yr but i'm fully remote so i accept it. kind of wish i had done law school instead.

62383520
>>62381049
Just bombed a senior product manager and coordinator interview.
I talk too much and don’t get to the point. That’s the direct feedback I got, which is true.

I have another offer I already accepted at a faang company though. It’s lower level and need to move though.

62383530
>>62383520
>It’s lower level and need to move though.
are you going to do it? i can't imagine moving for work in the year 2026, when these big indianjewnigger companies can (and do) blindly lay people off all the time.

62383536
>>62381049
100% stuck at a job I hate that boring as fuck most days and the few days where I actually need to work I bust my ass off all day because my retarded managers will fuck the schedule up and have only one person on to do three peoples worth of work. I lucked into a middle management position though in a field with basically no mobility so I can't really leave without taking a huge paycut. Make $36/hr which feels like nothing because I also live in a pretty expensive area.

62383538
>>62383096
Whatever you want anon. Start a business on the side or smthg.
>>62383530
I will because can’t take the current company anymore or I will Elliott Rodger someone in Minecraft.

62383574
>>62382822
Commerical and residential buildings in my state literally cannot be occupied if they don't pass a fire inspection

62383585
I deliver mail for 30 bucks an hour. Paid for 8.5 hours usually done in 5-6, it's a weird evaluated quazi salaried thing.

62384021
>>62381049
Early 40s, trapped in a flyover shithole making $80k in data analytics. Entry level title with a senior-level workload and enough experience to at least land interviews for data engineering roles with some regularity, though there are only a handful of openings in my area each year. I'm pretty frugal and on track to be able to semi-retire in Asia within a few years which is looking increasingly likely given the state of the job market and my career.

62384045
i'm a family doc and make ~130/hr. it sucks but at least my schedule is condensed into 4 days a week and i'm hoping to retire in 9 years.

62384057
>42
>middle manager but my job is going to be outsourced in the next year or so
>110k before taxes
>live in New York suburbs so it’s actually not as much money
I have a lot invested and saved so when I’m axed it’s tempting to just try to survive on that instead of working again. no family to support and I inherited the house I live in with no mortgage.

62384184
>$37/hour salary equivalent post taxes and $1k a month 401k contribution
is this good

62384198
>>62384184
Depends on CoL in your area, but it's better than most.

62384480
$50k cad customer support
36 yrs old
I am severely underperforming but I love making music -- married a woman from a well off family, we have a house
Confidence low, bros, confidence low

62384785
>>62384480
As it should be, you're the woman in the relationship.

62385873
>>62384785
Not exactly, but maybe to an extent as I am an artistic man. I'm definitely the defender though.

Not sure how to upgrade but also keep up with my artistry which I feel is my greatest passion and inspiration

At the very least I feel teaching your children music and related science opens up their mind to a lot of thinking

62385905
I make sandwitch

32 per hour aud 48 hr weeks

62388145
>>62381049
GSE Fleet mechanic for an airline 38$ an hour 60$ an hour for service calls

62388200
>>62381049
I'm 39m and a farmer. My dad retired about 10 years ago and I have taken over everything since then. Before then I had been working for him since I could walk pretty much. I remember the time before roundup ready beans and I spent most of my summers pulling weeds.

I made about $250k last year and my living expenses are about $10k. I live in town and own one of the cheapest houses in the county because my dad wouldn't let me live in a yurt in the grove. I drive a '78 F-150 I paid $400 for when I was in high school.

Anyways outside of planting and harvest I work about 3 hours per day and spend the rest of the day shitposting, checking my stocks, playing playstation 2, watching my sitcom DVD collection, and playing a round of golf at the local muni.

62388215
>>62381049
I make 60$ an hour to respond to passive aggressive emails
I got one waiting in my inbox rn.

62388275
$16 an hour at a dead end non-tech job even though I have a degree and some certs. I am the go to IT guy in my family which sometimes pays, the other day I installed a new hard drive and cleaned the pc for a family member. I didn't even ask for money since it was such a simple task but she gave me 60 bucks for it since that is what a store was going to charge her.

62388285
>>62381049
110k senior security consultant, wfh, unlimited pto, 4 years at the global company, burned out for the last 1.5 years but can't afford to quit because I got the job straight out of college through internship and I have no idea how to get another job because I refuse to do any certs or practice interviewing.

62388291
>>62388275
Why do you not have a job with your degree? Are you applying?

62388337
>>62388200
I envy the cheap pickups people can buy in farm country. on Long Island everything succumbs to rust almost immediately or is sold within the closed Spanish-speaking illegal community, so is impossible to buy.

item.title: How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened? — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened?

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-19 16:40 UTC

OP

How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened?

Early replies (first 3)

62387242
>>62387239
Who cares

62387246
>>62387239
Wendeez nuts hit your chin

62387248
>>62387239
Because GDP has always been a meme.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387300
Too busy raping kids

62387318
>>62387239
Federal job cuts and govt spending in key sectors, AI efficiency gains, imperialistic tariffs, deregulation. Basic economic theory. Everything the media said was bad was actually good for GDP growth

62387322
>jump up and down on the street in front of wumbo sized Wendy, arms flailing desperately over your head, making a complete fool of yourself
>her huge eyeballs shift down to meet your diminutive form
>WENDY! WENDY! HEY WENDYYYY!
>the sky darkens as her huge smug face takes up your whole vision
>I-I-I love Wendys! I get a Breakfast Baconator every morning on the way into work! And I doordash the spicy nuggets all the time, twenty piece! W-with a Frostie, those are the best!
>she stares you into the ground for a few seconds before laughing like a thunderclap above your head
>her hand moves to one of her huge, bus sized shoes and begins slipping it off
>"well, how about a free ride for my favorite customer~?"

62387388
europe is a bureaucratic hellhole, nothing happens without a massive amount of paper work, it's a nightmare for entrepreneurs, no wonder they can't into ai, all their researchers are in america

62387389
>>62387388
/thread

You can't even shoot someone killing you in Europe. Pathetic nations all around.

62387404
>>62387239
because 2/3 of their GDP dollar value comes from AI or AI related industry, the whole shebang is just make belief and it works as long as the rest of the world agrees to play along, which it currently does. But oh boy, the second the facade drops and everyone can see the corpse they're parading around as a savior of the economy..

62387420
>>62387388
My problem (as an entrepreneur) is that to get soft funding, you have to precisely fit a mould. For example, there are lots of small check options, but only if you're associated with this university, or that university. Or only if you're a woman. Or only if you're working on climateshit. As for hard funding, investors don't want to take risk. I saw unironically a French investor who claim they only invest in pre-seed, but only if you have 100k+ ARR and their max ticket size is 250k and they exclusively lead rounds (meaning max round size is 500k). How the fuck is this pre-seed then, and why the fuck would anyone take 250k if they are already at 100k ARR? That's mostly an exception, usually the problem is they can't fucking decide and then cry about it.
But American investors are far worse, they don't want to invest in anything that's not AI. "Sorry but we only do preseed if you have clients. Oh you have clients uh... b-but did you talk to a big player ye- you did and you have a written statement that they'll buy at a high price if you can implement feature X? uh (damn, how can I say no...) oh uh w-we only invest if you have at least 100 b2b clients all paying 50k+ [these are series B stats btw]"
Worst part is Americans and their fans keep claiming it's the opposite, that American investors try to find a reason to accept while Europeans try finding a reason to reject. I want to punch Paul Graham in the face everytime he opens his lying whore mouth about America, SF investors, or YC, everything he says is the opposite of reality. But that also applies to other Americans.

62387614
>>62387239
Why wouldn't it be?

* The US has far cheaper energy and chemical feedstocks.
* The US benefits from economies of agglomeration and network effects in important growth sectors such as information tech.
* The US isn't letting itself get bossed around by the US like the EU is, which makes the US look like a better place to invest.

62387618
Is it not the most obvious answer in the world, I mean really if you actually sit down and think about it for 2 milliseconds

62387621
>>62387239
I am european and I work like 4 hours a day and I spend my days strolling, drinking with friends and playing vidya.
That's how
we're europemaxxing

62388089
>>62387239
wtf do you mean "everything that's happened"?
you mean europe fostering an underclass of people who don't and wont work? you mean europe actively building ghettoes and cultivating race riots? you mean europe taxing and regulating all innovation away? you mean europe dismantling its power plants to chase fantasies?
europe crippled itself economically and there is no financial reason to ever start a company here over the us so most of the great startups leave

62388103
>>62387621
Sounds peaceful
Good luck with the foreign invasion

62388287
>>62388089
What about the tariffs?

62388362
>>62387239
Both the US and Europe are in decline, but both have very far to fall, in spite of acceleration. The US has always outperformed Europe economically, while Europe has mogged the US in history, cultural diversity, and art.

62388366
>>62388362
And science and tech. The pipeline is often that europe invents some pivotal tech but fails to monetize it because the infrastructure for this is dogshit in europe, so either some us company copies it and wins the market, the european company moves to the us, or a us company purchases the european company and markets the tech through the main office.

62388374
>>62388366
I agree but it's also the pipeline of the past. I'd also add that the US university research system is pretty good at science and tech discovery as well. But lately, a lot of the monetization and scaling manufacturing for non software tech discovered or invented in the US or Europe has come from China.

item.title: China may have got its hands on an ASML EUV machine — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
China may have got its hands on an ASML EUV machine

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-19 16:28 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62388111
>>62388106
This might mean China has obtained parts to reverse engineer. Or it might mean Chinese engineers have been making progress surprisingly fast and US spies think China must be cheating in some way. Either way, it signals progress.

62388147
>>62388106
>>62388111
No they didnt get one, Hauwei created their own! China numba 1!
They'll soon be shitting out all the chips. Nvidia will be fucked.
Tech sector in the Ass&Piss500 will tank. Chinese supremacy is encroaching on us.

62388154
>>62388106
If it means computer parts go down in price, idgaf. Hail China or whatever they say.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62388164
>>62388106
This is cope because Huawei figured out a way to stack chips in 3d (sort of like memory) and make enough progress without needing EUV.

62388165
>>62388147
this, but unironically

62388187
>>62388147
If they have an EUV machine to reverse engineer, it will speed up their development of a domestic EUV machine, not allow them to instantly finish development.

If they have an experimental EUV machine, it will still take years to improve it to the point that it is commercially viable, then also produce and to deploy it en masse.

Either way, it's still years until China has a proper domestic alternative to TSMC up and running.

62388189
>>62388154
Maybe in the 2030s

62388203
>>62388106
This is old news.. They hired a bunch of former ASML engineers involved in making the machines. Literally have a bunch of old Europeans dudes in a factory, making this thing.
Progress is slow, but they are getting there.. and we get to read about these "warnings" every time they pass a hallmark in the engineering.

62388217
>>62388111
Reuters reported in December that China had an EUV machine prototype in Shenzhen, that had not yet produced a working chip. So they might have got it to produce working chips.

62388229
>>62388147
I'm impressed by chinas innovation. Electric car with drone built into the roof, robotic electric car charging arm, swappable electric car battrerys, damn.

62388318
We can barely build the fucking things and we have full control of the supply chains and the inatructiions. It is actually known china has one of the early versions but zero support for it.

62388356
>>62388111
>Chinese engineers have been making progress surprisingly fast
Not a thing. Their culture requires copying all ideas, and these machines are notorious for being anti bug just like the ball point refiners.

item.title: Tick tock ADA-fags — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Tick tock ADA-fags

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-19 16:28 UTC

OP

Time's almost up.

Early replies (first 3)

62387098
>>62387094
This is now peak shitcoin measurement.

62387348
>>62387094
My bags are light because they have no value

62387364
>>62387094
ADA to $20 EOY

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387467
>>62387364
20c is doable

62387503
Little Charlie showed his true colors by taking a timeout when everything was going down. Is the price of the token truly all that matters to him? What about the giant ecosystem he was building?? top lel

62387504
>>62387503
Atleast Charles is honest. Segey is a sleazy Russian scammer who's been dangling carrots for 9 years. Hopefully he gets lynched by a baggie

62388063
>>62387503
The 900$ total usdt liquidity?

62388092
>>62387094
LINK flippin ADA will be the first step to redemption for the crypto market, ultimately ending with LINK flipping XRP. Only then will cryptofags stop being forsaken.

62388256
>>62387467
in zimbabwean dollars, yes, i agree

62388308
>>62388092
I used to think that ADA and XRP money would jump ship to LINK.
Now I think that when ADA and XRP eventually crashes and burns, the money will simply exit crypto entirely instead.

62388345
>>62388308
Crypto was a liquidity trap from 2016-2022,2023ish

AI is the new hotness and at least produces something somewhat tangible

62388355
>>62387094
lmao they're both slipping out of page 1.
in 10 years we'll have the same screenshots we do from 2014 with dogshit like dash, and other btc forks, but this time with LINK and ADA together, as they both belong to the same tier.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 79 replies — 42.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-06-19 14:50 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378439
>>62374533
Photonics sounds solid
I base all of the following off my schizo intuition:

1. I keep hearing about how Elon musk is a retard and SpaceX IPO is a rug pull. But you don't get that rich by being stupid.

2. I can't see X ever being profitable (though it is valuable for other reasons), and nobody gives a shit about electric cars anymore. But SpaceX... he is throwing money on that for a reason.

3. And Starlink is the world's biggest photonics deployment in space right now.
Satellite internet + photonics is going to be a huge deal
- cheap, super fast internet (terabits per second) that doesnt depend on today's bloated over-regulated infrastructure, and you can access it from anywhere
- Governments - specially EUcucks - keep making it difficult for you to use the internet and have free speech, could get BTFO the same way as the twitter trannies did when elon bought X
- Any retard shithole country with underwater drones can cut underwater internet cables, shooting down a satelite would be more difficult
- Perfect opportunity for a psyop to push people into using starlink instead of traditional intenet

If you couple that with AI companies also wanting photonics for their datacenters, you could get a lot of money this way

62378965
Brothers,
Just learn how to use the order book in polymarket, it's full of idiots that are willing to buy your bags for absurd prices. We can set our profile pictures to anime pic rel >62373866 to coordinate, target events like the current fifa event and mass shill or fud a team or group of teams so we can pump our bags
Two days ago i made 4K by just selling Belgium to win the world cup at 3x the actual price and some nigger actually bought it

62379061
>>62377886
Vague platitude

Option number 8 by >>62378965

There could be gains to be made on polymarket. Many of us are American so we'd either use kalshi or a vpn

The anon was so kind to give us an excerpt on his success and /biz/ has yet to make an impact on these markets like it did in 2021 in the BSC casino

By the end of the day, we shall have a decision set

62380691
Bump

62381251
Bump

62381343
>>62378002
/ic/ is filled with schizoids and endless seethe. And this is coming from someone who draws as a hobby.

62381797
>>62373752
>4 figure hell
unironically get a job, any job
like just work a year lol

62383107
It's settled, tune in tomorrow!

62383529
>>62373752
I am all in on SpaceX with my poorstack €6k

62383569
>>62373752
finally,a good general

62384962
Yes, leverage everything and bet it all on the moon shot. You won't regret it.

62384983
Patiently waiting for the hundred bucks of link I bought a decade ago to turn me into a millionaire.

Any day now.

62384990
>>62381797
I AM TRYING
BUT NOBODY IS HIRING

62384995
>>62378002
It's base policy to stop /ic/ from turning into a Patreon beggar spam board

62385082
>>62384990
Use your CPU to mine Nerva/XNV then. A properly decentralized crypto with no mining pools. Even a laptop works!

62385259
I bought 3 shares of spacex at 172 each should I kill myself

62385391
>>62381343
Would you like to join a group of /biz/tards who also took a liking to drawing as a hobby?
>>62384995
Yeah that makes sense, although they do have a dedicated thread to shill their blogs which would conveniently link up to any Patreon.

62385458
>>62385259
why are you chasing hype

62385552
>>62385458
not smart

62385679
>>62373888
Well video games suck ass now. But we have AI which is supposed to help make projects like that easier. Godot is a free open source video game engine that is supposedly easy to use. Why don’t we learn how to do that?

62385716
>>62385391
>Would you like to join a group of /biz/tards who also took a liking to drawing as a hobby?
Sure

62385756
>>62385716
https://archive.4plebs.org/pol/thread/528268175/#528271334

Two links are in the archive one is for shitposting and the other is more art related it's the only way to share them here.

62385759
>>62383107
Ok, so you said it’s settled. What is the decision? Btw if your current mascot doesnt work out I nominate that we use anime Elizabeth Holmes as our mascot. Plus she better exemplifies us anyways.

62385818
34 with 8k to my name
Should I just give up? Honest question

62386248
I have a negative NW of $45k at 31. Unemployed. I don’t know how it all went so wrong for me. I don’t know how to fix it. I have a degree but it doesn’t do shit for me. Worst part is that I had almost $300k 4 years ago lmao.

62386520
>>62386248
How did that happen

62386827
>>62385759
Call me strange but I prefer looking at the real thing.

That being said we'll stick to the two most posted anime girls for the mascot.

62386828
>>62386827
>>62385759

62387408
>>62385259
Just hodl, I also have 4 shares but I also have a nw in 6 figures mainly in index fund and cash interest accounts, I treat it as a mid term to long term gamble. It may be massively overvalued but markets can go full retard for the stupidest of reasons. Just remember to take initial out with some profits if it does.

item.title: TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN — 31 replies — 23.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-19 15:24 UTC

OP

TUMBLING DOWN TUMBLING DOWN

Early replies (first 3)

62381997
>>62381962
Can't he just take the dividend and up it to like 20%? That should drive the price back up because people like free money right? What's stopping him?

62382004
>>62381997
not going to help if he's not going to maintain peg

62382061
To bad Saylor can't release a stable coin to pump BTC that would require DeFi lol. Tom Lee however will mention this in the next shareholder meeting.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382284
>>62382061
btc to 7k
shtlink 50.....cent - HAHAHAHA

62382319
>>62381962
the bottom is unironically $0

62382323
>>62382250
Yeah, 32 years if he sells bitcoin, but I bet that doesn't add up to the panic he will cause by a massive sell off, also liquidity, I doubt any market is ready for this retard to dump 32billion in bitcoin.

Btw, he has maybe 7-8 months of cash left to pay for dividends if he doesn't sell bitcoin or doesn't dillute his shares.

I think we can all see where this is heading to

62382325
>>62382323
54* billion

62382328
>>62382323
>7-8 months
bear market will be over by then

62382337
>>62382328
>the bear market will be over despite the biggest holder being underwater and forced to sell soon
yeah sure anon i can just FEEL the bull fomo

62382345
>>62381962
and I don't think anyone ever commented this here properly, but the impact in other protocals which relies mostly of their liquidity pool in bitcoin will be huge, imagine once people would start selling off their bitcoin or trading to any stablecoin, that will depeg as well. We had just a briefly taste of that in Luna, some other protocols that relied in Wrapped Luna in their holdings, I recall USDT, USDC depegging for a brief time and a random protocol depegging pretty bad.

I don't think people realize how in the shit this market will be if he fucks up hard.

62382352
>>62382328
I don't know, we are heading to 2 rate hikes this year and a bubble worse than .dot bursting at the same time. I don't feel comfortable at all to say that this 4 year cycle will keep untouched.

62382359
>>62382352
dotcom*

62384528
>>62382345
Usdt and usdc are backed by fixed income instruments, not saylors money magic, which like UST, is basically a derivative of Bitcoin/MSTR

62384592
>>62381997
The dividend IS almost 20% at that price lmao.

62384600
>>62381997
And pay the dividends with what money? He would have to offload a shitton of BTC. That's the crux of the issue

62384605
WERE SO BACK

62384738
>>62384600
It's called a refi, retard

62384752
>>62384600
>And pay the dividends with what money?
Saylor can go get a loan to pay for it. Ever heard of a bank?

62384761
>>62384738
>>62384752

62384809
>people bought an income product from michael saylor
DO GOYIM REALLY?

62384820
>>62381962
> Perpetual stretch
Lmao cant make this shit up

62385164
>>62381997
I believe that would just accelerate the already existing issue if the price were to continue to stay the way it is.

62385261
>>62381997
>>62385164
So let me explain what happens, once dividend raise he has more obligations to pay, that has to come from somewhere and it's from his credit. Now he has 4 means to pay his dividends from credit: 1- loans; 2 - Cash in reserve (1.1b); 4- dilluting his stocks; or 4: selling bitcoin (53B). The issue is: he has barely 9 months left to pay the CURRENT dividend yield, which I guess he accounted for something like 12% at that time. He either increses his debts (which is possible but again, refinance where?) or sell bitcoin. the problem about selling his shares of MSTR is that it both dillutes the bitcoin per share (which is good) at a cost of his shareholders, which are going to dump the price even further. The problem is that at the same time he WANTS to buy more bitcoin and it's using part of that dillution cash, so in the end mNAV (value of bitcoin per share) is not attractive to the regular investor which could simply buy Bitcoin. So basically down this path he is destroying his common stock and the premium of btc/share and exposure.

The second path which is selling bitcoin only worsens the whole ecosystem, drags price down, his reserves devalue agressively.

So that's it, both selling MSTR and BTC end up in a death spiral and both can and are happening at the same time.

62385316
>>62385261
Bitcoin is a swarm of cyber hornets.

62385351
Saylor redeemed right here and magically STRC went up.

62386426
>>62382328
That’s around the time that we should be at the bottom of this bear market. Bitcoin is going to around $35,000

62386451
>>62381997
REFINANCE WHERE MICHEAL

62388118
WHADDAMMA GONNA DO?!?!
IT'S TIME TO
BIIIIIIIIIIITTTCCOOOOOOONNNNEEEECCCCT

62388124
The world needs energy, Bitcoin is digital energy, which makes saylor the modern day John d. Rockefeller

62388198
>>62385261
That's a lot of words to say Saylor has no income and is fucked unless BTC pumps.

item.title: >govt collects tax >its not enough >issues bonds providing interest for money >needs more money next year >tax isnt enou… — 15 replies — 16.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>govt collects tax >its not enough >issues bonds providing interest for money >needs more money next year >tax isnt enou…

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-19 15:49 UTC

OP

>govt collects tax
>its not enough
>issues bonds providing interest for money
>needs more money next year
>tax isnt enough
>issues bonds providing interest for money
>did this so many times now need debt to pay back debt

why not just NOT spend like a high end escort?

Early replies (first 3)

62379039
>>62379035
OP is a bulgarian faggot who spams this board and scams people.

62379041
>>62379039
im black and i dont spam this board

62379042
>>62379035
>why not just NOT spend like a high end escort?
the austerity necessary to balance the budget would cause an armed revolt.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379044
>>62379035
>>62379042

62379046
>>62379035
If you are referring to the US, one of the reasons for this is there was a greater appetite for dollars outside the US than in it. However, things have changed since the dollar system was politicized.

62379058
I want the fed to raise rate to 35% and destroy the world.

thoughts?

62379071
>>62379041
worse, kys nigger

62379101
money exists to be spent. if you don't spend, there are no jobs, there is no development, there are no goods and services. the purpose of an economy is to produce things, not to hoard money. we can already see that the "free market", left to its own devices, prefers to hoard rather than spend, and uses hoarded resources to oppress and stifle growth rather than encourage it

62379304
>>62379044
Hmm those corporate taxes seem kinda low

62380467
>>62379035
Until jfk, we didn't have a deficit (he was the first one to cut taxes for economic growth). Even then, it took until Reagan for tax cuts to turn into a massive problem, with him basically halving them and double national debt in his time. Trump did the same thing (rich nigga tax cuts) and it tripled national debt, then that problem doubled under biden. The government has been collecting more of your taxes and less taxes of the people who actually have money to foot the bill. This shit is what caused the french revolution; king louis said, "we're broke dead fucking poor. Nobles, foot the bill". The Nobles said, "BITCH I'D RATHER DIE" and then they did.

We're already stripped every neccesary and not neccesary government organization and service to the bone. We can't even have free-tax filing; the problem isn't cost, it's revenue.

62384846
>>62379035
The money printer is the opiate of the state. The planet is addicted to the Rothschild smack.

62385524
>>62379304
>taxes
>too low
Go glow somewhere else
Instead how about we stop giving all our fucking money away in foreign aids and dem programs while removing all income taxation

62386402
>>62385524
We can have social services AND have lower taxes for the average person. Just put back corporate tax rates back to what they were during the boomer's time and simplify the tax code.

62386422
>>62385524
Foreign aid is basically nothing. The biggest expenditures are Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, and VA benefits. Basically everything else is a rounding error.

62388264
>>62386402
>just increase taxes
No, they're already beyond exorbitant
We don't need all these social services just to import the difference and make everybody worse off
>>62386422
>basically nothing
Good, then it won't be missed when it gets cut
>social security
>medicare
>medicaid
Can all be cut as well. We don't need these funds simply to be "borrowed against" to collateralize even more bullshit the government doesn't need to be involved in.
>inb4 oh noes what about the nigger riots!?
Arrested and put to work. 2 for 1
>va benefits
Are just about the only legit thing there, since its upholding the bargain of a citizen giving their life and livelihood to the state for a time in return for perks later in life
I long for the day the state has outward facing teeth instead of inward again

item.title: Hello everyone! I'm sorry if this has already been asked before, since i'm new here. I would like to get into crypto Is… — 4 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone! I'm sorry if this has already been asked before, since i'm new here. I would like to get into crypto Is…

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-19 16:11 UTC

OP

Hello everyone!


I'm sorry if this has already been asked before, since i'm new here.


I would like to get into crypto


Is XRP coin a good one to buy?


I was also looking into the Bitcoin, but it's too expensive for me.


Ethereum is also an option, since it costs much less than a Bitcoin, but still multiple thousands.


I'd figure with XRP coin I would have better chances, since if that one goes to $2000 or even $60.000 I would make a shitload of money.


At this point I could at least get multiple XRP coins.


Thanks all for the help!


Appreciate it.

Early replies (first 3)

62387721
>>62387635
>XRP
bullish

62388253
>>62387635
>I'd figure with XRP coin I would have better chances, since if that one goes to $2000
Yep, would recommend buying at least 50-100, Japanese banks are gonna build their stablecoins on the XRP ledger.

62388276

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62388317
I can't wait to break even on my XRP. Boom!

62388223

Just a tiny dip = just the tip.

1. SPCX has 2 valuable businesses but is waaaay overpriced
2. SPCX has all the AI CAPEX a the top of the bubble, bigger than its actual business
3. SPCX is aimed at stupid retailers and retards with stuff like datacentres in space
4. Anybody buying it right now is either retarded, or gambling on buy high sell higher
5. It's going to be lower in the near future
6. It's going to be a great purchase once it trades at 1/6th its price
7. The girl in OP's picture is hot, and
8. >>62387652 that's the way we like it.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 42 replies — 131.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-06-19 16:23 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383263
>>62383255
Post your holdings and your hand

62383265
I'm selling in 2028/2029. I still remember back in 2021 when LINK was $45 and I kept debating with myself whether to sell or not and decided not to. That shit is going to haunt me for the rest of my life.

62383267
>>62383255
>>the price is bad because they are supressing it
yes, see pic

>>sure they dump 17 million tokens and rebuy 100k of them
We've known about the supply unlocks for 9 years, we've known about the buybacks for less than a year

>swift and dtcc are going live without the "necessary" decentralized oracle network
Every single Chainlink product uses the decentralized oracle network. What a bizarre thing to just lie about.

62383269
>>62383263
>no i totally don't hold the token, i just know everything about it to more accuratelly fud
*cries in third world*

62383274
>>62383267
>the switch bro's, the switch is flipped
>surely the price must be pushing higher from all these onchain buy fees towards the link token
>wait
>why is everything a cash deal between that company and cll
>why is cll paying their own nodes

62383275
>>62383274
>>why is cll paying their own nodes
Because cll runs the contract.

Holy shit you're retarded

62383279
>>62383275
You see this lurkers? Another layer of cope comes forth.

62383287
>>62383279
I love how you always pretend you have this huge silent audience. This board is dead, maybe 15 people will ever see your posts, and 14 of them will instantly know you're a clueless retard.

62383291
>>62383287
I love how you always pretend you have this answer for brownian motion. This board is an echo chamber, maybe 15 people will ever see your posts, and 14 of them will instantly know you're the same as them.

62383298
>>62383291
>same as them
>same skin color as them
Sorry, I want to add that to the end of my post. Makes it way funnier.

62383299
Ram my pussy bitches

62383312
>>62383299
Sorry femanon, the marines have been beating their dicks senseless to anime jpegs for years. I don't think they can keep it up for a real woman. They'll probably cum too fast and watch some bull finish you.

62383932
>>62382813
I'v been waiting for SWIFT, DTCC, and the whole banking system to come online with tokenization using Chainlink, just like everyone else has. And we're very close, anon. Can you smell it? Smell it.

62387308
>>62383932
I can smell your poverty kek

62387313
>>62383312
Now this is some real fuddie cuck fanfiction. Bravo.

62387314
>>62387313
Link marines are unironic cuckolds who get off seeing Sergey dump hundreds of millions of tokens on Binance. They've been waiting 9 years for their "real life" to begin kek

62387379
>>62383932
This. Just 2 more years and we will all make it

62387714
>>62382813
I've been holding since september or november 2017, bought at 30c average sold 2/3 of my original stack between $38 and $12, still holding the rest, but there is nobody I despise more than idiots defending "the team" and coping, I know many of you retards bought at $5 or even higher and are sweating right now, and that's a good thing, you don't deserve anything better than the current price action. Instead of giving Chainlink the heat they deserve you idiots defend them. At least cocksuckers like that chainlinkgod retard or the catfishy catfish idiot get paid for doing damage control, but you absolute miserable fucks do it for free

62387730
>>62382825
the memories of defi summer are still fresh in my mind. we really had fun, didn't we

62387778
In all these years nobody could ever explain what Chainlink is even supposed to be doing without the explanation devolving into a buzzword ouroboros.

62387876
>>62382825
I just wish you would discuss your beanie babies somewhere else

62387900
>>62387778
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4hR4rDbJPM4

62387912
>>62387876
Stop making threads then

62387923
>>62387912
I have made as many threads about the topic as chainlink has intrinsic value. (0)

62387994
>>62387714
You sound pretty angry.

62388115
Let's be honest. You'd do the same in Sergeys position. Some annoying faggots that won't shut the fuck up online shitting up your nice official social media posts for years on end?

Yeah it is absolutely unfair that these crypto companies get away with basically fucking over retail by offering stocks with 0 of the benefits and protections of being a stockholder but you'd absolutely do the same in his position keeping your cards close since you can since nothing is forcing you to give any information out. Project seems to be in a good place but we all expected an eth run in 2021 and never got it and been absolutely destroyed for 5 straight years. Every day for me I wake up and I hate my dumb fucking life but I'll still kys before I ever sell

62388131
>>62388115
>it is absolutely unfair that these crypto companies get away with basically fucking over retail by offering stocks with 0 of the benefits and protections of being a stockholder
so why didn't you buy stocks instead? Are you some kind of retard?

62388141
>>62387778
It's got the fastest tps and it's going to replace swift (kaboom)

62388326
>>62382813
First time recently I have sold link in over years.
All the trades I did recently outperformed my LINK holdings for the past 5 years, it's been time I let it go, at this point I have more hope for Eth than LINK.

62388343
>>62388115
They deserves 1000x worse. How about they square up their tokenomics, respect their own truth > trust ethos, and close up their Cayman office now that Clarity is all but signed? As you said, their hands are deep in the honey pot. Like everything happening in the West, people complain a bit and do absolutely nothing, so they continue while payrolling their Wumao dogs like clg and swathes of barely obfuscated LINK-branded bot accounts to induce apathy.

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 79 replies — 26.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-06-19 13:42 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382804
>>62381740
Are you serious nigger?

62382895
buying when boomers capitulate and then riding it all the way to the top must be one of the best feelings ever, but somehow i always end up not buying because I always get lured by bears into waiting for "bettery entry prices".

62383146
>>62382799
Not really. Being a store of value is already a use case. Some people are just interested in whether BTC can be used for more than buying, holding, and eventually selling.

62383366
>>62383146
Where do you do that?

62383375
>>62382895
Just buy today and you will thank in 10 years

62383385
>>62377444
it has

62383588
>>62383366
DYOR on Babylon and you'll see.

62383598
>>62371718
I will still hold it

62383603
>>62382895
How many btc do you hold?

62383619
>>62371718
Sunk cost fallacy. I think I'm retarded. If bitcoin does moon I'm still retarded, but a lucky retard.

62383732
>>62383619
We gonna be rich

62383734
>>62383588
Low apr and kinda not worthy

62383743
>>62375554
Etf is even better

62384166
>>62383734
Sure, it's not life-changing yield, but for some people it's better than letting their BTC sit idle.

62384175
>>62383743
If all you want is price exposure, sure. A lot of people still prefer owning the actual asset.

62384208
Where is satoshi?

62384223
>>62377200
It's all about hype and doesnt have any value

62384235
>>62372468
You better trade memecoins for that

62385618
>>62384235
Go for it. Nobody's stopping you, anon

62386979
>>62384208
Nobody knows

62387122
>>62384166
Do you think it will attract a lot of interest in the long run? The model looks good, and since it's self-custodial while expanding Bitcoin's use cases, I don't think it raises the usual concerns that many people have with DeFi.

62387125
>>62383619
Anon is relaxing

62387534
>>62387122
We'll know for sure once TBV launches on mainnet. That's when we'll see if it gains adoption and makes a real impact.

62387549
>>62383732
If you buy memecoins, not $btc

62387563
>>62387534
Defi is no longer valuable

62387566
Im fine

62387833
>>62387563
Depends what you mean by valuable. A lot of the hype is gone, but people are still borrowing, lending, and farming yield.

62387836
>>62387549
Doge coins lol

62387875
>>62371718
Because it's the only crypto asset I'd be comfortable holding for the next decade without constantly checking the chart.

62387892
the longer this market exists, the more Bitcoin seems to become the benchmark everything else gets compared against. So very very bullish

item.title: NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS… — 77 replies — 19.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!! THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!! SOMEONE MUS…

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-06-19 12:44 UTC

OP

NO NO NO NO NO NO!!!!!!!!!!

THIS SIMPLY CANNOT BE! ELON MUSKRAT IS A CONMAN, SPACEX MERELY A PONZI SCHEME!!!

SOMEONE MUST PUT A STOP TO THIS NOW!!!!!!!!!!!! REDDIT ARMY RISE UP!!!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62375140
this is entirely sustainable, you have EDS if you disagree

62375150
>>62375140
Short it then, stupid nigger

62375163
>company called "Space Exploration Technologies"
>run by the first trillionaire

>you STILL didn't buy any of their stock

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379451
>>62375140
mathematically impossible valuation.

62379484
>>62375255
Your instinctive contrarianism is why you belong to the permanent underclass

62379507
>>62375841
Anti-natalists are mentally ill and project their retardation onto others.

62379519
>>62377191
tbf I like grok more than gemini, copilot, or deepseek. Not sure yet how I think it stacks up to claude or chatgpt though.

62381795
>>62375140
how to gotted so much dollars?

62381800
>>62375128
Imagine believing 2 weeks represents absolutely anything about the viability or future trajectory of a stock. No wonder you guys get annihilated so regularly.

62381830
>>62377191
SpaceX performs 60% of the world's space launches, which is 95% of the American market. If they aren't a space company, then there isn't a space company that exists at all.

62381991
>>62381830
>He thinks (((outer space))) is like in the movies and what they teach in (((schools))) and show on your (((television)))
Cute. You need to be 18 to post here, champ.

62382075
>>62375140
>>62375255
Unlike faggy AI, space is an actual next frontier of human development.
While indians are busy generating piles of poo here on earth to jerk off to, real MEN will be mining asteroids in space and speculating on the Asteroid Belt stock exchange

62382118
my parents aped in and told me they didn't want to get left behind lol. Load zee 60% dump.

62382338
>>62382075
>real MEN will be mining asteroids

62382719
>>62382075
>Unlike faggy AI, space is an actual next frontier of human development.

SpaceX won't fucking EXIST by the time we get there.

62382732
>>62382118
That's really sad :(

62382961
>>62379484

62384212
>>62375140
Musk haters BTFO

People fail to realize that it's not just SpaceX. It's X.com, it's xAI, It's X payments. It's literally everything internet related. People can hate on Musk all they want, but he will own the internet and all internet related activity including purchasing, social media will go through his companies. Enjoy staying poor

62384226
>>62384212
>X.com, it's xAI, It's X payments
so it's facebook. although I like xAI, half of their stuff is good on the AI scale but sourcing data from twitter is a mistake.

62384237
>>62384226
It could have been Zuck, but he's straight up unlikable and wasted so much time and money on the wrong tech (VR) while everyone else moved to AI

62384247
Lol @ this thread
Elon is a weird incel

62384277
SpaceKeks

62384281
>>62375128
OP ARE YOU OK??

62384293
kwab

62384467
>>62381830
SpaceX is an AI company. Rockets are a literal hobby when you look at the specifics of the valuation. It’s like buying a house in order to get the Twinkie sitting inside the fridge.

62384596
bought at 170 sold at 203 how low it can go before I slurp back in

62385059
>>62375393
>IF Elon can deliver on what he claims, then this stock is cheap.
Data centers in space arent worth it though. Its massively more expensive and doesnt even make it easier for the user.

There is no space business outside of gov contracts and a few sat companies.

62385083
>>62375128
behold the power of autism and stock manipulation. kek.

62385509
>>62385059
The benefit of data centers in space is that is exponentially increases the cost and technical headaches while heavily limiting the ability of the consumer to utilize it

62387344
>>62385509
Also you can host child porn on them and no jurisdiction can reach you cause you're in orbit

62387424
>>62385509

they need orbital AI because it reduces latency, which is critical for defense; its
much better to have the data center in space when calculating missile interception, for instance.

62387496
Its going to be the same playbook. Pump in the initial first days, dump after the unlocks happen, and slowly creep back up after a years worth of dumping.

62387710
I bought 30 shares, am I gonna make it?

item.title: Uh? This will have consequences — 36 replies — 19.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Uh? This will have consequences

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-19 14:50 UTC

OP

https://www.usdebtclock.org/

Are you all just shit coin investors? Did any of you even bother to do the math on this?

Literally, the US is going to enter into debt insolvency at about 55 trillion. That will happen pretty fast.

What does this have to do with business?

Well, the US has lost HALF of it's money's value in the last 25 years. That is going to get exponentially worse.

I get it, twenty years ago, people reeeee'd about "meh hyper inflations"... but seriously, the hour glass is losing sand fast.
|
The public debt will become your problem eventually.

Early replies (first 3)

62384457
wrong

62384460
>>62384445
>This will have consequences
cope

62384462
wen doe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384584
>>62384460
>the empire will never collapse
>because it just wont ok
kek lmao even

62384611
>>62384460
Retard. I bet if you asked the Roman Empire back then if they thought they would ever collapse they would probably think you are insane. Prepare now.

62384703
>>62384570
if you believe a market correction is tantamount to "the end of the world" then it only goes to show how raped the economy truly is and that you've internalized this fact without even realizing

62384734
>>62384703
I didn't say anything about a market correction nigger, i'm trying to say people have been calling for the end of the world forever and it never happens. despite doomer niggers screeching the world has gotten better over time for the average person

62384788
>>62384734
Let's talk math. Given that the US must roll over a third of its near-zero interest debt to market rates this year, and given current trends in Federal spending and revenue, and given current interest rates, the interest on the debt ALONE will exceed all Federal revenue in about ten years. And that is a conservative estimate because it does not take into account waning interest in US treasury paper, the loss of revenue from the current recession, and the fact that interest rates will almost certainly begin climbing rapidly as the deadline approaches. So what then? There is no political will to make the drastic cuts in spending that would be needed to turn the ship around. When the USA starts paying the mortgage with a credit card, the MMT scam is finished. Not the end of the world, but the end of the American empire. That is for sure.

62384816
>>62384788
I’ve been hearing this for 20 years

62384818
>>62384445
>1pbtid
so sad

62384829
>>62384445
>the large amount of existing dollars will have consequences
???
if this number was 0 it'd mean that nobody is using dollars

62384972
>>62384734
>i'm trying to say people have been calling for the end of the world forever and it never happens.
The OP didn’t say anything about the world ending though

62385662
>>62384788
>blocks your path

62385676
>>62384445
>The public debt will become your problem eventually.
it wont
unless you keep bagholding fiat

62385763
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62385793
>>62384445
>Burgerland
Who gives a fuck about that shit hole country?

62386424
>>62384788
>There is no political will to make the drastic cuts in spending that would be needed to turn the ship around.
DeSantis and some friends are going around the country trying to convince state legislatures to pass a constitutional balanced budget amendment without Congress.
There's a surprising number of states that have already signed on.

62386723
>>62384445
Day traders and nepobabies don't care because they reckon it's a criminal's economy and nobody will ever do anything about it anymore.

Maybe they're right.

62386736
>>62384445
The debt is already insolvent.

62386773
>>62386424
I wish them luck. Seriously. But I'm betting it won't happen.

62386776
>>62385662
Now is not really the optimal moment for rattling the American saber, seeing as how a bunch of rag head are twisting Trump's nut sack with impunity. Really think they are going to bully the whole world into using toilet paper for money? Those days are over.

62386859
>>62384445
The solution is very simple and everyone knows it. You know what it is too.

62386860
>>62384445
just roll it forward

62386870
>>62386424
None of that means anything unless the core problem is addressed. Government spending is only an issue after that fact.

62387790
>>62384570
it did

62387805
i dont hear anyone complaining that the government doesnt pay them. ever thought about that? its meaningless. there is no creditor

62387808
you could set it to $0 and its effectively the same. in fact that's the plan

62387956
>>62387808
I think you should do this

62387969
>>62384818
im on mobile and my id constantly changes
i imagine this is most posters
just realize this is the world now

62387975
At least fort knox still has gold in it
Geg

62388041
>>62386776
You have Trump Derangement Syndrome. Iran lost.

62388070
>>62388041
>TACO
>b-b-but they lost!
trump doesn't care if you polish his turds
the war was a bad idea on the merits, but this surrender is a fucking joke
